summary: Excellent matrix transfer captures a fire‑filled show highlighted by a monster Mississippi Half‑Step/Franklin’s Tower opener and a second set full of standout jams.
review: The recording, painstakingly mixed by Hunter Seamons, is a crystal‑clear matrix that balances audience ambience with a full‑bodied band sound. Musically the concert shines – the half‑hour opening jam of Mississippi Half Step into Franklin’s Tower, a ripping Miracle > Bertha, a funky Saint of Circumstance, and a killer Stella Blue close out a second set that many fans call “one for the ages.” Brent’s Rhodes piano and synth textures are repeatedly praised, and Jerry’s lead work is described as monumental despite a brief early‑set strain.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high praise for both performance (standout jams, strong set flow) and recording quality (full‑bodied matrix mix); weighted rating yields a near‑5‑star score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, full‑bodied balance with audience noise well‑controlled; minor leveling issue at the very start of Half Step but overall clear and warm throughout.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A marathon opening jam of Half Step into Franklin’s Tower sets a high‑energy tone, followed by a mix of cowboy/C&W flavored tunes and an explosive second set that delivers experimental yet upbeat jams.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His lead work is called “monumental arena rock” and his tone perfect; a brief early‑set strain is noted but quickly resolves.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob’s El Paso > Mexicali segment is highlighted as fun, especially for cowboy‑heart fans.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s Rhodes piano is front‑and‑center on most tracks and his synth textures receive high praise; described as a key element of the show’s energy.
